chapter_title.en: Chapter on Endowments, Charity, and Gifts

text: 5592 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَانَ يَرُدُّ اَلنِّحْلَةَ فِي اَلْوَصِيَّةِ وَ مَا أَقَرَّ عِنْدَ مَوْتِهِ بِلاَ ثَبَتٍ وَ لاَ بَيِّنَةٍ رَدَّهُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.5592 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni: "Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), peace be upon him, used to reject gifts (grants) in a will, and whatever a person acknowledged at the time of his death without proof or evidence; Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) would reject it."
